revision.comment: 新しいページ：隅田八幡宮古墳（和歌山県橋本市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 隅田八幡宮古墳 |画像 = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 隅田八幡塚古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]隅田町中島354 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 19|緯度秒 = 57.30 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 38|経度秒 = 39.43 |ISO = JP-30 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径16m
高さ5.5m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]代 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 橋本市指定史跡「隅田八幡塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 隅田
八幡宮古墳 |ラベル位置 = left }} '''隅田八幡宮古墳'''（すだはちまんぐうこふん、隅田八幡塚古墳）は、[[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]隅田町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。橋本市指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「隅田八幡塚古墳」）。 == 概要 == 和歌山県北部、[[紀の川]]中流北岸の段丘上（標高約130メートル）に築造された古墳である。現在は東約100メートルに[[隅田八幡神社]]が所在する。[[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）に実測調査が実施されている。 墳丘の周囲は流失しているが、元々の墳形は円形と見られ、直径約16メートル・高さ約5.5メートルを測る{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南東方向に開口する。[[緑泥片岩]]の切石を使用した直方体状の整備な石室で、構築に際してはL字形組み合わせ・繰り込み石挿入による精巧な技術が認められる。石室内の副葬品は詳らかでない。築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]代と推定される。 古墳域は[[1968年]]（昭和43年）に橋本市指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「隅田八幡塚古墳」）。 === 来歴 === * [[江戸時代]]後期、『[[紀伊続風土記]]』霜山城跡の項に「岩屋」として記載。 * [[1968年]]（[[昭和]]43年）5月28日、橋本市指定史跡に指定（指定名称は「隅田八幡塚古墳」）。 * [[1972年]]（昭和47年）、実測調査（西山要一・松田正昭・久貝健）{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。 * 玄室：長さ約3.3メートル、幅約1.7メートル、高さ1.7メートル * 玄門：幅約1.3メートル、高さ約1.5メートル * 羨道：現存長さ約1.5メートル 石室の石材は[[緑泥片岩]]。玄室の奥壁では、高さ1.3メートルの巨石1枚の上に切石5個を積み上げる。側壁は1段目に大石を据え、その上に切石を4・5段垂直に隙間なく積む。石室の構築に際しては、L字形組み合わせ・繰り込み石挿入による精巧な技術が用いられ、一部に漆喰の塗布が認められる。また床面には、かつて一面に6-10センチメートルの石が敷かれていたという。天井石は、玄室では3枚、羨道では2枚。石室前には、閉塞石と見られる巨石が残る。石室は古くから開口していたと見られ、副葬品は詳らかでない{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。 Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|石室俯瞰図 Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|墳丘 == 文化財 == === 橋本市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 隅田八幡塚古墳 - 1968年（昭和43年）5月28日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=八幡宮古墳|title=和歌山県史 考古資料|publisher=和歌山県|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|和歌山県史 考古資料|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=隅田八幡塚古墳|title=橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|publisher=橋本市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=八幡宮古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 31 和歌山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|八幡宮古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=辻林浩|year=1989|chapter=八幡宮古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|八幡宮古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Suda Hachimangu Kofun}} * [[隅田八幡神社]] {{デフォルトソート:すたはちまんくうこふん}} [[Category:和歌山県の古墳]] [[Category:橋本市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定史跡]]

墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 隅田八幡塚古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]隅田町中島354 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 19|緯度秒 = 57.30 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 38|経度秒 = 39.43 |ISO = JP-30 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径16m

八幡宮古墳 |ラベル位置 = left }} '''隅田八幡宮古墳'''（すだはちまんぐうこふん、隅田八幡塚古墳）は、[[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]隅田町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。橋本市指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「隅田八幡塚古墳」）。 == 概要 == 和歌山県北部、[[紀の川]]中流北岸の段丘上（標高約130メートル）に築造された古墳である。現在は東約100メートルに[[隅田八幡神社]]が所在する。[[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）に実測調査が実施されている。 墳丘の周囲は流失しているが、元々の墳形は円形と見られ、直径約16メートル・高さ約5.5メートルを測る{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南東方向に開口する。[[緑泥片岩]]の切石を使用した直方体状の整備な石室で、構築に際してはL字形組み合わせ・繰り込み石挿入による精巧な技術が認められる。石室内の副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]代と推定される。和歌山県では[[岩内1号墳]]（[[御坊市]]）と並んで切石積みの石室を有する数少ない古墳として重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|八幡宮古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。 古墳域は[[1968年]]（昭和43年）に橋本市指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「隅田八幡塚古墳」）。 === 来歴 === * [[江戸時代]]後期、『[[紀伊続風土記]]』霜山城跡の項に「岩屋」として記載。 * [[1968年]]（[[昭和]]43年）5月28日、橋本市指定史跡に指定（指定名称は「隅田八幡塚古墳」）。 * [[1972年]]（昭和47年）、実測調査（西山要一・松田正昭・久貝健）{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。 * 玄室：長さ約3.3メートル、幅約1.7メートル、高さ1.7メートル * 玄門：幅約1.3メートル、高さ約1.5メートル * 羨道：現存長さ約1.5メートル 石室の石材は[[緑泥片岩]]。玄室の奥壁では、高さ1.3メートルの巨石1枚の上に切石5個を積み上げる。側壁は1段目に大石を据え、その上に切石を4・5段垂直に隙間なく積む。石室の構築に際しては、L字形組み合わせ・繰り込み石挿入による精巧な技術が用いられ、一部に漆喰の塗布が認められる。また床面には、かつて一面に6-10センチメートルの石が敷かれていたという。天井石は、玄室では3枚、羨道では2枚。石室前には、閉塞石と見られる巨石が残る。石室は古くから開口していたと見られ、副葬品は詳らかでない{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。 Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|石室俯瞰図 Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|墳丘 == 文化財 == === 橋本市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 隅田八幡塚古墳 - 1968年（昭和43年）5月28日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=八幡宮古墳|title=和歌山県史 考古資料|publisher=和歌山県|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|和歌山県史 考古資料|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=隅田八幡塚古墳|title=橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|publisher=橋本市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=八幡宮古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 31 和歌山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|八幡宮古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=辻林浩|year=1989|chapter=八幡宮古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|八幡宮古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Suda Hachimangu Kofun}} * [[隅田八幡神社]] {{デフォルトソート:すたはちまんくうこふん}} [[Category:和歌山県の古墳]] [[Category:橋本市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定史跡]]

八幡宮古墳 |ラベル位置 = left }} '''隅田八幡宮古墳'''（すだはちまんぐうこふん、隅田八幡塚古墳）は、[[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]隅田町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。橋本市指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「隅田八幡塚古墳」）。 == 概要 == 和歌山県北部、[[紀の川]]中流北岸の段丘上（標高約130メートル）に築造された古墳である。現在は東約100メートルに[[隅田八幡神社]]が所在する。[[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）に実測調査が実施されている。 墳丘の周囲は流失しているが、元々の墳形は円形と見られ、直径約16メートル・高さ約5.5メートルを測る{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南東方向に開口する。[[緑泥片岩]]の切石を使用した直方体状の整備な石室で、構築に際してはL字形組み合わせ・繰り込み石挿入による精巧な技術が認められる。石室内の副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]代と推定される。和歌山県では[[岩内1号墳]]（[[御坊市]]）とともに数少ない切石積みの石室を有する点で重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|八幡宮古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。 古墳域は[[1968年]]（昭和43年）に橋本市指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「隅田八幡塚古墳」）。 === 来歴 === * [[江戸時代]]後期、『[[紀伊続風土記]]』霜山城跡の項に「岩屋」として記載。 * [[1968年]]（[[昭和]]43年）5月28日、橋本市指定史跡に指定（指定名称は「隅田八幡塚古墳」）。 * [[1972年]]（昭和47年）、実測調査（西山要一・松田正昭・久貝健）{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。 * 玄室：長さ約3.3メートル、幅約1.7メートル、高さ1.7メートル * 玄門：幅約1.3メートル、高さ約1.5メートル * 羨道：現存長さ約1.5メートル 石室の石材は[[緑泥片岩]]。玄室の奥壁では、高さ1.3メートルの巨石1枚の上に切石5個を積み上げる。側壁は1段目に大石を据え、その上に切石を4・5段垂直に隙間なく積む。石室の構築に際しては、L字形組み合わせ・繰り込み石挿入による精巧な技術が用いられ、一部に漆喰の塗布が認められる。また床面には、かつて一面に6-10センチメートルの石が敷かれていたという。天井石は、玄室では3枚、羨道では2枚。石室前には、閉塞石と見られる巨石が残る。石室は古くから開口していたと見られ、副葬品は詳らかでない{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。 Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|石室俯瞰図 Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|墳丘 == 文化財 == === 橋本市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 隅田八幡塚古墳 - {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 隅田八幡宮古墳 |画像 = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 隅田八幡塚古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]隅田町中島354 |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 19|緯度秒 = 57.30 |経度度 = 135|経度分 = 38|経度秒 = 39.43 |ISO = JP-30 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径16m
高さ5.5m |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]代 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 橋本市指定史跡「隅田八幡塚古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Wakayama |アイコン = 円墳 |ラベル = 隅田
八幡宮古墳 |ラベル位置 = left }} '''隅田八幡宮古墳'''（すだはちまんぐうこふん、隅田八幡塚古墳）は、[[和歌山県]][[橋本市]]隅田町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。橋本市指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「隅田八幡塚古墳」）。 == 概要 == 和歌山県北部、[[紀の川]]中流北岸の段丘上（標高約130メートル）に築造された古墳である。現在は東約100メートルに[[隅田八幡神社]]が所在する。[[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）に実測調査が実施されている。 墳丘の周囲は流失しているが、元々の墳形は円形と見られ、直径約16メートル・高さ約5.5メートルを測る{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南東方向に開口する。[[緑泥片岩]]の切石を使用した直方体状の整備な石室で、構築に際してはL字形組み合わせ・繰り込み石挿入による精巧な技術が認められる。石室内の副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]代と推定される。和歌山県では[[岩内1号墳]]（[[御坊市]]）とともに数少ない切石積みの石室を有する点で重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|八幡宮古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。 古墳域は[[1968年]]（昭和43年）に橋本市指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「隅田八幡塚古墳」）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[江戸時代]]後期、『[[紀伊続風土記]]』霜山城跡の項に「岩屋」として記載。 * [[1968年]]（[[昭和]]43年）5月28日、橋本市指定史跡に指定（指定名称は「隅田八幡塚古墳」）。 * [[1972年]]（昭和47年）、実測調査（西山要一・松田正昭・久貝健）{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南東方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。 * 玄室：長さ約3.3メートル、幅約1.7メートル、高さ1.7メートル * 玄門：幅約1.3メートル、高さ約1.5メートル * 羨道：現存長さ約1.5メートル 石室の石材は[[緑泥片岩]]。玄室の奥壁では、高さ1.3メートルの巨石1枚の上に切石5個を積み上げる。側壁は1段目に大石を据え、その上に切石を4・5段垂直に隙間なく積む。石室の構築に際しては、L字形組み合わせ・繰り込み石挿入による精巧な技術が用いられ、一部に漆喰の塗布が認められる。また床面には、かつて一面に6-10センチメートルの石が敷かれていたという。天井石は、玄室では3枚、羨道では2枚。石室前には、閉塞石と見られる巨石が残る。石室は古くから開口していたと見られ、副葬品は詳らかでない{{Sfn|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}。 Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|石室俯瞰図 Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|墳丘 == 文化財 == === 橋本市指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 隅田八幡塚古墳 - 1968年（昭和43年）5月28日指定。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 地方自治体発行 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=八幡宮古墳|title=和歌山県史 考古資料|publisher=和歌山県|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|和歌山県史 考古資料|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=隅田八幡塚古墳|title=橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|publisher=橋本市|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|橋本市史 民俗編・文化財編|2005}}}} * 事典類 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=八幡宮古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 31 和歌山県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|八幡宮古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=辻林浩|year=1989|chapter=八幡宮古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|八幡宮古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Suda Hachimangu Kofun}} * [[隅田八幡神社]] {{デフォルトソート:すたはちまんくうこふん}} [[Category:和歌山県の古墳]] [[Category:橋本市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:和歌山県内の市町村指定史跡]]

{{Japanese Ancient Tomb | Name = Suda Hachimangu Kofun | Image = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other Name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | Belongs to = | Location = {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | Latitude Deg = 34|Latitude Min = 19|Latitude Sec = 57.30 | Longitude Deg = 135|Longitude Min = 38|Longitude Sec = 39.43 | ISO = JP-30 | Shape = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|chlorite schist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: * Length: Approximately 3.3 meters * Width: Approximately 1.7 meters * Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: * Width: Approximately 1.3 meters * Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: * Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|chlorite schist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: * Length: Approximately 3.3 meters * Width: Approximately 1.7 meters * Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: * Width: Approximately 1.3 meters * Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: * Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suda Hachimangu Kofun | image = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | Belongs to = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | Latitude Deg = 34|Latitude Min = 19|Latitude Sec = 57.30 | Longitude Deg = 135|Longitude Min = 38|Longitude Sec = 39.43 | ISO = JP-30 | Shape = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: * Length: Approximately 3.3 meters * Width: Approximately 1.7 meters * Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: * Width: Approximately 1.3 meters * Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: * Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the back wall) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Mound == Cultural Properties == === Hashimoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun — Designated on May 28, 1968. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Local Government Publications * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture History - Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | Belongs to = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | Latitude Deg = 34|Latitude Min = 19|Latitude Sec = 57.30 | Longitude Deg = 135|Longitude Min = 38|Longitude Sec = 39.43 | ISO = JP-30 | Shape = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: * Length: Approximately 3.3 meters * Width: Approximately 1.7 meters * Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: * Width: Approximately 1.3 meters * Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: * Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: * Length: Approximately 3.3 meters * Width: Approximately 1.7 meters * Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: * Width: Approximately 1.3 meters * Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: * Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: * Length: Approximately 3.3 meters * Width: Approximately 1.7 meters * Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: * Width: Approximately 1.3 meters * Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: * Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: * Length: Approximately 3.3 meters * Width: Approximately 1.7 meters * Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: * Width: Approximately 1.3 meters * Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: * Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|publisher=Hashimoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}}} * Encyclopedia * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture Place names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 31|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koji Tsujibayashi|year=1989|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Japanese Kofun Dictionary|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Hashimoto City]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Circular Tombs]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Municipal Designated Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Suda Hachimangu Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Suda Hachimangu Kofun]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] {{Kofun navbar}} [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Suda Hachimangu Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Suda Hachimangu Kofun]][[ja:隅田八幡宮古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:横穴式石室]]

The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: * Length: Approximately 3.3 meters * Width: Approximately 1.7 meters * Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: * Width: Approximately 1.3 meters * Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: * Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: * Length: Approximately 3.3 meters * Width: Approximately 1.7 meters * Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: * Width: Approximately 1.3 meters * Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: * Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: * Length: Approximately 3.3 meters * Width: Approximately 1.7 meters * Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: * Width: Approximately 1.3 meters * Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: * Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: * Length: Approximately 3.3 meters * Width: Approximately 1.7 meters * Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: * Width: Approximately 1.3 meters * Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: * Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suda Hachimangu Kofun | image = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | Belongs to = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | Latitude Deg = 34|Latitude Min = 19|Latitude Sec = 57.30 | Longitude Deg = 135|Longitude Min = 38|Longitude Sec = 39.43 | ISO = JP-30 | Shape = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: * Length: Approximately 3.3 meters * Width: Approximately 1.7 meters * Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: * Width: Approximately 1.3 meters * Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: * Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the back wall) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Mound == Cultural Properties == === Hashimoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site * Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun — Designated on May 28, 1968. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suda Hachimangu Kofun | image = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | Belongs to = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | Latitude Deg = 34|Latitude Min = 19|Latitude Sec = 57.30 | Longitude Deg = 135|Longitude Min = 38|Longitude Sec = 39.43 | ISO = JP-30 | Shape = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: * Length: Approximately 3.3 meters * Width: Approximately 1.7 meters * Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: * Width: Approximately 1.3 meters * Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: * Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suda Hachimangu Kofun | image = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | Belongs to = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | Latitude Deg = 34|Latitude Min = 19|Latitude Sec = 57.30 | Longitude Deg = 135|Longitude Min = 38|Longitude Sec = 39.43 | ISO = JP-30 | Shape = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: * Length: Approximately 3.3 meters * Width: Approximately 1.7 meters * Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: * Width: Approximately 1.3 meters * Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: * Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suda Hachimangu Kofun | image = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | Belongs to = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | Latitude Deg = 34|Latitude Min = 19|Latitude Sec = 57.30 | Longitude Deg = 135|Longitude Min = 38|Longitude Sec = 39.43 | ISO = JP-30 | Shape = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: * Length: Approximately 3.3 meters * Width: Approximately 1.7 meters * Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: * Width: Approximately 1.3 meters * Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: * Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suda Hachimangu Kofun | image = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | Belongs to = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | Latitude Deg = 34|Latitude Min = 19|Latitude Sec = 57.30 | Longitude Deg = 135|Longitude Min = 38|Longitude Sec = 39.43 | ISO = JP-30 | Shape = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the back wall) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Mound == Cultural Properties == === Hashimoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun — Designated on May 28, 1968. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}}
