{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suda Hachimangu Kofun | image = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | Belongs to = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | Latitude Deg = 34|Latitude Min = 19|Latitude Sec = 57.30 | Longitude Deg = 135|Longitude Min = 38|Longitude Sec = 39.43 | ISO = JP-30 | Shape = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the back wall) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Mound == Cultural Properties == === Hashimoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun — Designated on May 28, 1968. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} Materials|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Wakayama Prefecture History|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun|title=Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|publisher=Hashimoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture Place names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 31|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koji Tsujibayashi|year=1989|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Japanese Kofun Dictionary|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}} revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suda Hachimangu Kofun | image = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | Belongs to = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | Latitude Deg = 34|Latitude Min = 19|Latitude Sec = 57.30 | Longitude Deg = 135|Longitude Min = 38|Longitude Sec = 39.43 | ISO = JP-30 | Shape = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the back wall) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Mound == Cultural Properties == === Hashimoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun — Designated on May 28, 1968. == References == Materials|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Wakayama Prefecture History|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun|title=Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|publisher=Hashimoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture Place names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 31|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koji Tsujibayashi|year=1989|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Japanese Kofun Dictionary|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}}== See Also == * {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}} revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suda Hachimangu Kofun | image = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | Belongs to = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | Latitude Deg = 34|Latitude Min = 19|Latitude Sec = 57.30 | Longitude Deg = 135|Longitude Min = 38|Longitude Sec = 39.43 | ISO = JP-30 | Shape = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | Dimensions = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the back wall) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Mound == Cultural Properties == === Hashimoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun — Designated on May 28, 1968. == References == History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|publisher=Hashimoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture Place names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}} 31|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koji Tsujibayashi|year=1989|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Japanese Kofun Dictionary|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}} Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | Map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | Label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | Label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | Construction Era = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label Position = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the back wall) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Mound == Cultural Properties == === Hashimoto City Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun — Designated on May 28, 1968. == References == Kofun|title=Japanese Kofun Dictionary|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}} Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | coordinates Deg = 34|coordinates Min = 19|coordinates Sec = 57.30 | coordinates Deg = 135|coordinates Min = 38|coordinates Sec = 39.43 | ISO = JP-30 | Shape = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | Excavated Artifacts = | Associated Tombs = | built = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | Historical Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, Kofun|title=Japanese Kofun Dictionary|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}} Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | coordinates = 34|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 57.30 | coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 39.43 | ISO = JP-30 | type = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | Burial Facility = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = | Associated Tombs = | built = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | other_designation = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original shape is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Cultural Properties Volume|2005}}. sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. == Burial Facility == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. Height: 5.5m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = | Associated Tombs = | built = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | other_designation = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suda Hachimangu Kofun | image = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | coordinates = 34|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 57.30 | coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 39.43 | ISO = JP-30 | type = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = | Associated Tombs = | built = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | other_designation = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | Notes = | map = Japan Wakayama | Icon = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the back wall) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Mound == other_designation == === Hashimoto City Designated other_designation === * Historic Site ** Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun — Designated on May 28, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == other_designation Volume|publisher=Hashimoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite 31|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koji Tsujibayashi|year=1989|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Japanese Kofun Dictionary|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}} It is designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designated as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City other_designation on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designation1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designation1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City other_designation on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's dimensions are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suda Hachimangu Kofun | image = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | coordinates = 34|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 57.30 | coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 39.43 | omitted = JP-30 | type = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | burial goods = | Associated Tombs = | built = 7th century | Buried Individual = | Tomb Designation = | other_designation = Hashimoto City Designated other_designation, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | Cultural Property = | notes = | map = Japan Wakayama | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is designation1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was designation1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City other_designation on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_designation Volume|2005}}. Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto City|ja|橋本市}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is other_designation1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|ja|紀の川}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|ja|隅田八幡神社}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_other_designation Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was other_designation1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City other_other_designation on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_other_designation Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_other_designation Volume|2005}}: * Burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and other_other_designation Volume|2005}}. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter.
