Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | part_of = | location = [[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]], Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | coordinates = 34|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 57.30 | coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 39.43 | omitted = JP-30 | type = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enfun (circular tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = | Associated Tombs = | built = 7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb designation1 = | Historic Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Wakayama | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in [[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]], Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kinokawa River]] in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the [[:en:Suda Hachiman Shrine|Suda Hachiman Shrine]]. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|1=Iwauchi Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80362924}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}: * burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward the back wall) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun — Designated on May 28, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Local Government Publications ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|publisher=Hashimoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture Place names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 31|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koji Tsujibayashi|year=1989|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Japanese Kofun Dictionary|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Suda Hachiman Shrine|Suda Hachiman Shrine]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|隅田八幡宮古墳|version=100827900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:隅田八幡宮古墳]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] {{Commons category|Suda Hachimangu Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q124322411}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q124322411}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|1=Iwauchi Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80362924}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}: * burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in [[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]], Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kinokawa River]] in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the [[:en:Suda Hachiman Shrine|Suda Hachiman Shrine]]. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|1=Iwauchi Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80362924}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}: * burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in [[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]], Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kinokawa River]] in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the [[:en:Suda Hachiman Shrine|Suda Hachiman Shrine]]. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|1=Iwauchi Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80362924}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}: * burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in [[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]], Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kinokawa River]] in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the [[:en:Suda Hachiman Shrine|Suda Hachiman Shrine]]. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|1=Iwauchi Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80362924}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}: * burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward the back wall) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun — Designated on May 28, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Local Government Publications ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture History - Archaeological Materials|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Wakayama Prefecture History|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun|title=Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|publisher=Hashimoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture Place names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 31|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koji Tsujibayashi|year=1989|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Japanese Kofun Dictionary|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * [[:en:Suda Hachiman Shrine|Suda Hachiman Shrine]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|隅田八幡宮古墳|version=100827900|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:隅田八幡宮古墳]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] {{Commons category|Suda Hachimangu Kofun}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q124322411}}

The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in [[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]], Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kinokawa River]] in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the [[:en:Suda Hachiman Shrine|Suda Hachiman Shrine]]. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|1=Iwauchi Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80362924}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}: * burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in [[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]], Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kinokawa River]] in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the [[:en:Suda Hachiman Shrine|Suda Hachiman Shrine]]. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|1=Iwauchi Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80362924}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}: * burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}: * burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suda Hachimangu Kofun | image = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]

Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in [[:en:Hashimoto, Wakayama|Hashimoto City]], Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the [[:en:Kinokawa River|Kinokawa River]] in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the [[:en:Suda Hachiman Shrine|Suda Hachiman Shrine]]. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|1=Iwauchi Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80362924}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]]: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}: * burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. [[co:commons:Category:Suda Hachimangu Kofun]][[ja:隅田八幡宮古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Suda Hachimangu Kofun]][[ja:隅田八幡宮古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Suda Hachimangu Kofun]][[ja:隅田八幡宮古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q124322411]] Wikidata name: Suda Hachimangū Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:33, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suda Hachimangu Kofun | image = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|en|Hashimoto, Wakayama|WD=Q842098|lt=Hashimoto City}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | coordinates = 34|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 57.30 | coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 39.43 | omitted = JP-30 | type = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enfun (circular tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = | Associated Tombs = | built = 7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb designation1 = | Historic Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Wakayama | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|en|Hashimoto, Wakayama|WD=Q842098|lt=Hashimoto City}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|en|Kinokawa River|WD=Q1042624|lt=Kinokawa River}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|en|Suda Hachiman Shrine|WD=Q10961321|lt=Suda Hachiman Shrine}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|1=Iwauchi Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80362924}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}: * burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward the back wall) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun — Designated on May 28, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Local Government Publications ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture History - Archaeological Materials|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Wakayama Prefecture History|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun|title=Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|publisher=Hashimoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture Place names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 31|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koji Tsujibayashi|year=1989|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Japanese Kofun Dictionary|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|en|Suda Hachiman Shrine|WD=Q10961321|lt=Suda Hachiman Shrine}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|隅田八幡宮古墳|version=100827900|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:隅田八幡宮古墳]] {{Commons category|Suda Hachimangu Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q124322411}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|en|Hashimoto, Wakayama|WD=Q842098|lt=Hashimoto City}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|en|Kinokawa River|WD=Q1042624|lt=Kinokawa River}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|en|Suda Hachiman Shrine|WD=Q10961321|lt=Suda Hachiman Shrine}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|1=Iwauchi Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80362924}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}: * burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward the back wall) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun — Designated on May 28, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Local Government Publications ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture History - Archaeological Materials|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Wakayama Prefecture History|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun|title=Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|publisher=Hashimoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture Place names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 31|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha}}|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koji Tsujibayashi|year=1989|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Japanese Kofun Dictionary|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|en|Suda Hachiman Shrine|WD=Q10961321|lt=Suda Hachiman Shrine}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|隅田八幡宮古墳|version=100827900|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:隅田八幡宮古墳]] {{Commons category|Suda Hachimangu Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q124322411}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Suda Hachimangu Kofun]][[ja:隅田八幡宮古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Suda Hachimangu Kofun]][[ja:隅田八幡宮古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Suda Hachimangu Kofun]][[ja:隅田八幡宮古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Suda Hachimangu Kofun | image = [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|en|Hashimoto, Wakayama|WD=Q842098|lt=Hashimoto City|1=Hashimoto, Wakayama}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | coordinates = 34|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 57.30 | coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 39.43 | omitted = JP-30 | type = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enfun (circular tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter: 16m
Height: 5.5m | burial = Both-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu (horizontal stone chamber)|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = | Associated Tombs = | built = 7th century | Interred_individual = | Tomb designation1 = | Historic Site = Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site, "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun" | designation1 = | notes = | map = Japan Wakayama | type = 円墳 | map_dot_label = Suda
Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|en|Hashimoto, Wakayama|WD=Q842098|lt=Hashimoto City|1=Hashimoto, Wakayama}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|en|Kinokawa River|WD=Q1042624|lt=Kinokawa River|1=Kinokawa River}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|en|Suda Hachiman Shrine|WD=Q10961321|lt=Suda Hachiman Shrine|1=Suda Hachiman Shrine}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=Greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|1=Iwauchi Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80362924}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}: * burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=Greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (view toward the back wall) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Corridor (view toward the entrance) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Corridor (view toward the burial chamber) Suda Hachimangu Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance Suda Hachimangu Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Mound == Historic Site == === Hashimoto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun — Designated on May 28, 1968. == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * Local Government Publications ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture History - Archaeological Materials|publisher=Wakayama Prefecture|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Wakayama Prefecture History|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2005|chapter=Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun|title=Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|publisher=Hashimoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture Place names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 31|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koji Tsujibayashi|year=1989|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Japanese Kofun Dictionary|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|en|Suda Hachiman Shrine|WD=Q10961321|lt=Suda Hachiman Shrine|1=Suda Hachiman Shrine}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|隅田八幡宮古墳|version=100827900|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:隅田八幡宮古墳]] {{Commons category|Suda Hachimangu Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q124322411}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|1=Iwauchi Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80362924}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The chamber's area are as follows{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}: * burial chamber: ** Length: Approximately 3.3 meters ** Width: Approximately 1.7 meters ** Height: 1.7 meters * Entrance portal: ** Width: Approximately 1.3 meters ** Height: Approximately 1.5 meters * Corridor: ** Remaining length: Approximately 1.5 meters The chamber was constructed using {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=Greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}}. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. Folklore and Historic Site Volume|publisher=Hashimoto City|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}}} * Encyclopedia ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Wakayama Prefecture Place names|series={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} 31|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=458249031X|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Koji Tsujibayashi|year=1989|chapter=Hachimangu Kofun|title=Japanese Kofun Dictionary|publisher=Tokyo-do Publishing|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|Hachimangu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|en|Suda Hachiman Shrine|WD=Q10961321|lt=Suda Hachiman Shrine|1=Suda Hachiman Shrine}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|隅田八幡宮古墳|version=100827900|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:隅田八幡宮古墳]] {{Commons category|Suda Hachimangu Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q124322411}} [[Category:History of Hashimoto, Wakayama]] [[Category:Kofun of Wakayama prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Mound and stone chamber entrance | Other name = Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|en|Hashimoto, Wakayama|WD=Q842098|lt=Hashimoto City|1=Hashimoto, Wakayama}}, Suda Town Nakajima 354, Wakayama Prefecture | coordinates = 34|coordinates = 19|coordinates = 57.30 | coordinates = 135|coordinates = 38|coordinates = 39.43 | omitted = JP-30 | type = {{ill|Enfun (circular tomb)|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enfun (circular tomb)|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter: 16m

Hachimangu Kofun | map_dot_label = left }} '''Suda Hachimangu Kofun''' (隅田八幡宮古墳, also known as Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun) is a circular tomb located in {{ill|Hashimoto, Wakayama|en|Hashimoto, Wakayama|WD=Q842098|lt=Hashimoto City|1=Hashimoto, Wakayama}}, Suda Town, Wakayama Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." == Overview == The tomb is situated on a terrace along the northern bank of the {{ill|Kinokawa River|en|Kinokawa River|WD=Q1042624|lt=Kinokawa River|1=Kinokawa River}} in northern Wakayama Prefecture, at an elevation of approximately 130 meters. About 100 meters east of the site is the {{ill|Suda Hachiman Shrine|en|Suda Hachiman Shrine|WD=Q10961321|lt=Suda Hachiman Shrine|1=Suda Hachiman Shrine}}. A detailed survey was conducted in 1972. Although parts of the mound have eroded, its original type is believed to be circular, with a diameter of about 16 meters and a height of 5.5 meters{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. The burial facility is a well-crafted, both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber that opens to the southeast. The chamber was constructed using cut {{ill|greenschist|ja|緑泥片岩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/greenschist|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 緑色片岩|1=Greenschist|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q917402}} stones, showcasing sophisticated techniques like L-shaped stone fittings and inserted stones for reinforcement. The chamber's grave goods are not well-documented. The tomb dates to the terminal phase of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, estimated to be from the 7th century. Its construction using cut stone makes it a notable site in Wakayama Prefecture, alongside the {{ill|Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|ja|岩内1号墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanai No. 1 Kofun|1=Iwauchi Kofun Group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q80362924}} in Gobo City{{Sfn|Hachimangu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}. The tomb site was Historic Site1 as a historic site by Hashimoto City in 1968. == Historical Timeline == * Late {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}: Mentioned as "Iwaya" in the "霜山城跡" section of {{ill|Kii Zoku Fudoki|ja|紀伊続風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kii Zoku Fudoki|qq=}}. * 1968: Designated as a Hashimoto City Historic Site on May 28 under the name "Suda Hachiman-zuka Kofun." * 1972: Detailed survey conducted by Yoichi Nishiyama, Masaaki Matsuda, and Ken Kugai{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}. == burial == [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Stone chamber aerial view}}]] [[File:Suda Hachimangu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber unfolded diagram}}]] The burial facility is a both-sleeved horizontal stone chamber opening to the southeast. The back wall of the burial chamber consists of a large stone (1.3 meters high) with five cut stones stacked on top. The side walls feature a base layer of large stones topped with four to five layers of tightly fitted cut stones. Techniques such as L-shaped joints and stone insertion were used for precision, with traces of plaster visible in some sections. The floor was once covered with stones measuring 6–10 centimeters. The ceiling consists of three stones in the burial chamber and two in the corridor. In front of the chamber, a large stone thought to be a sealing stone remains. The chamber appears to have been opened in ancient times, and the contents of the grave goods are unknown{{Sfn|Hashimoto City History - Folklore and Historic Site Volume|2005}}.
